fact: Over 40,000 GitHub issues analyzed from 6 frameworks
Frameworks: OpenClaw, AutoGen, CrewAI, LangGraph, Codex, Claude Code
OpenClaw #12442: cross-channel blocking, 6+ hours, 6+ unresponsive episodes
OpenClaw #25992: zombie subagents, 11+ min lane hold
OpenClaw #39282: agent amnesia, agent wrote own issue
OpenClaw #24031: contextTokens limit violations
OpenClaw #28629: 6.3GB RAM crash from unbounded logs
OpenClaw #3181: rate limit cascade from heartbeat loop
Problem classes: Scheduling (blocking, zombies, cascades, starvation) and Context (growth, amnesia, mismatch, waste)
Core insight: agent resources analogous to OS resources
Three contributions: empirical study, architecture, evaluation
Introduction claims 0 vs 27 zombies - tables show 0 vs 1 (normal), 7 vs 29 (high-load)
Introduction claims 45.2 vs 45.1 throughput - not found in any table
Abstract 86% P95 reduction matches faulty scenario (77524 vs 562771)
Abstract 168% throughput matches faulty scenario (11.0 vs 4.1)
All 3 baseline schedulers produce identical zombie counts and lane waste per scenario
Sliding Window worst context retention: 6.4% at 200 turns
AgentRM-CLM quality 0.95 vs baselines 0.87 consistently
OS analogy limitations: semantic value, coarse granularity, external dependencies
Agent frameworks assume unlimited resources - no contention, failure, or overflow handling
Cluster managers (Borg, K8s, YARN) handle infrastructure; AgentRM handles agent-level resources
LLM serving systems (vLLM, Orca, SGLang) optimize inference; AgentRM optimizes orchestration
